patches.find: ±10 nT geomagnetic variations at 9 INTERMAGNET stations during the April 8, 2024 total solar eclipse, under quiet geomagnetic conditions (Kp ≤ 3).
patches.replace: ±10 nT geomagnetic variations at 9 INTERMAGNET stations during the April 8, 2024 total solar eclipse, under quiet geomagnetic conditions (Kp ≤ 3). The GFZ Potsdam Kp archive corroborates this quiet-conditions reporting: the 3-hour Kp values across April 7–9, 2024 peak at 3.3, and during eclipse totality over North America (≈15:00–21:00 UTC, April 8) Kp ran 0.0–1.3. The dome author later disputed this (see update below), citing Kp ~5–6 as grounds for withdrawing the WIN, but that figure does not match the GFZ Potsdam record or the Dst index (which did not fall below −25 nT over the same window).
patches.rationale: EXP-238 P1: GFZ Potsdam Kp audit corroboration. Reconciles ISS-1482 c4 H2 Kp inconsistency by anchoring the Kp ≤ 3 claim to independent authoritative data. Kp peak 3.3 across April 7–9, eclipse-hour Kp 0.0–1.3 per GFZ Potsdam 3-hour archive.

patches.find: citing geomagnetic storm contamination during the April 8, 2024 eclipse (Kp ~5–6).
patches.replace: citing geomagnetic storm contamination during the April 8, 2024 eclipse (author-stated Kp ~5–6; see the GFZ Potsdam audit in detail evidence — the official 3-hour Kp peaked at 3.3 across April 7–9 and ran 0.0–1.3 during eclipse totality).
patches.rationale: EXP-238 P2: Attribution + cross-reference. Keeps dome author's stated number visible while flagging that independent Kp records contradict it.

patches.find: This is commendable scientific self-correction: the dome author withdrew a claim when data quality proved insufficient.
patches.replace: At first glance this looks like scientific self-correction: the dome author withdrew a claim he no longer felt confident in. On inspection, though, the stated reason (Kp ~5–6) does not match the independent Kp record — the official GFZ Potsdam peak across April 7–9, 2024 is 3.3, and eclipse-hour Kp is 0.0–1.3 — so the withdrawal rests on a factually incorrect premise rather than a newly discovered data-quality problem.
patches.rationale: EXP-238 P3: Charitable-then-honest framing. Credit withdrawal-as-action while not endorsing the falsified Kp rationale. Matches 'commendable on its face, problematic on inspection' pattern used elsewhere.

WIN-069 c5 H1 (major): Huber & Rust 2016 was published in The Stata Journal vol.16(2) pp.416-423 (DOI 10.1177/1536867X1601600209), not R Journal. Same citation-integrity error class as ISS-1467 Giacomin venue fix. Verified via SAGE Journals, EconPapers, Semantic Scholar.

patches.find: 6.2% RMSE is orders of magnitude worse than sub-meter geodesy; same-hemisphere routes (e.g. Australia to UK) also diverge at 6.2%, which V13 predicts should be its best case. AGD66→GDA94 shift is routine datum improvement, not evidence of topology
patches.replace: 6.2% RMSE is ~5 orders of magnitude worse than sub-meter geodesy; same-hemisphere routes (V13 best case) still show 10.2% mean error. AGD66→GDA94 shift is routine datum improvement, not evidence of topology
patches.rationale: WIN-069 c5 H2 (major): Finding had two errors: (1) 'Australia to UK' is cross-hemispheric (UK is Northern Hemisphere, Australia is Southern), not same-hemisphere as stated; (2) both cases were attributed the same 6.2% RMSE, but the patched c5 evidence distinguishes 6.2% cross-equatorial RMSE vs 10.2% mean error for same-hemisphere routes. New text uses the correct 10.2% for same-hemisphere and removes the geography error.

patches.find: A prediction and its cryptographically-timestamped duplicate, both registered after the only triggering event, cannot establish prospective status no matter the hash.
patches.replace: A prediction and its cryptographically-timestamped duplicate, both registered after the only triggering event, cannot establish prospective status no matter the hash (see the Timestamp Error tab for our full analysis of the dome's OTS methodology).
patches.rationale: WIN-061 c6 H2 (minor): Wire Timestamp Error tab cross-reference onto the cryptographically-timestamped-duplicate sentence, as recommended by curmudgeon. One parenthetical addition, no other changes.

patches.find:
The dome gets the base frequency wrong by 2.8×, then claims credit for perturbations around it. See Section 2.1.

V51.1 Update:
patches.replace:
The dome gets the base frequency wrong by 2.8×, then claims credit for perturbations around it. See Section 2.1. Independently, WIN-038 documents that the pole-only cavity floor H(0)=8,537 km gives f = 8.78 Hz, above 7.83 Hz at every radius — a second Self-Contradicted basis that does not depend on the averaging choice.

V51.1 Update:
patches.rationale: WIN-061 c6 H3 (minor): Wire WIN-038 pole-only floor cross-reference at end of paragraph 1, as recommended by curmudgeon. Strengthens the argument with an independent Self-Contradicted basis. Verbatim from curmudgeon stronger_arguments.

patches.find: Note the dome's account contains a structural contradiction: paragraph 1 cites the dome's own Live Power Dashboard reporting no G3+ storm observed since deployment, while this paragraph describes the dome's narrative of ~35% suppression during an observed G3+ storm. The March 20–22 2026 G3 event (NOAA SWPC confirmed, Kp=7) either triggered the monitor or it did not — the dome simultaneously presents both framings without resolving the discrepancy.
patches.replace: Note the dome's account contains a structural contradiction: paragraph 1 cites the dome's own Live Power Dashboard reporting no G3+ storm observed since deployment, while this paragraph describes the dome's narrative of ~35% suppression during an observed G3+ storm. The March 20–22 2026 G3 event (NOAA SWPC confirmed, Kp=7) either triggered the monitor or it did not — the dome simultaneously presents both framings without resolving the discrepancy. The dome's copper-firmament claim (WIN-034) compounds the difficulty: copper conductivity is invariant under G3-storm particle precipitation energies, so the boundary-side cavity response cannot supply a dome-specific suppression mechanism — any storm-time SR effect must be ionospheric, and per paragraph 1 the standard ionospheric response is mixed-direction.
patches.rationale: WIN-061 c6 H4 (minor): Append WIN-034 copper firmament mutual-exclusion sentence to end of V51.1 Update paragraph, as recommended by curmudgeon. Exact wording from c6 H4 recommendation.

patches.find: Non-discriminating: Chapman (1933) ionospheric mechanism + Kim & Chang (2018) predict the 39-eclipse ensemble signal magnitude and geometry.
patches.replace: Non-discriminating: the Chapman–Sq ionospheric framework (Chapman 1919, with eclipse-specific application in Rastogi 1962 and modern conductivity treatment in Yamazaki & Maute 2021) together with the Kim & Chang (2018) ensemble predicts the 39-eclipse signal magnitude and geometry.
patches.rationale: WIN-068 c12 H2 (minor): ISS-1491. Finding cited Chapman (1933) which is the lunar-tide paper; Chapman's eclipse-Sq work is his 1919 Phil. Trans. A 218 paper. Rewrites finding opener with correct attribution chain: Chapman 1919 → Rastogi 1962 → Yamazaki & Maute 2021.

patches.find: The dome's ensemble of 39 eclipses spanning 1991–2016 (Kim & Chang 2018) confirms this ionospheric mechanism; it does not require or validate dome cavity dynamics.
patches.replace: Kim & Chang (2018) compiled a 39-eclipse (1991–2016) INTERMAGNET ensemble that the dome cites as WIN-068; the ensemble confirms the ionospheric Sq mechanism but does not require or validate dome cavity dynamics.
patches.rationale: WIN-068 c12 H3 (minor): tldr_evidence said 'The dome's ensemble of 39 eclipses' — the 39-eclipse ensemble is Kim & Chang's, not the dome's. The dome cites it. Fix attribution to prevent a dome defender claiming 'we never assembled that data'.

patches.find: The dome's Steelman Evidence Ladder places eclipse geomagnetic evidence at Tier 1, explicitly acknowledging it is consistent with both models — an admission that WIN-068 alone cannot distinguish dome cavity dynamics from standard ionospheric physics.
patches.replace: See the verdict panel for the dome's own Tier-1 Steelman admission, which concedes this WIN alone cannot distinguish cavity dynamics from standard ionospheric physics.
patches.rationale: WIN-068 c12 ISS-1442: Verbatim duplicate sentence appears in both detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text. Curmudgeon recommendation: keep verdict-side (stronger rhetorical weight), replace evidence-side with a brief cross-reference. Resolves ISS-1442.

patches.find: the refraction fix leaves physical sun altitude indeterminate
patches.replace: the n(r) refractive contamination (Section 1.8 Coordinate Collapse) leaves physical sun altitude indeterminate
patches.rationale: WIN-068 c12 ISS-1443: Pre-rename terminology 'refraction fix' predates EXP-222/HNOTE-REFRACT-RESTRUCTURE-001 renaming. Updated to post-restructure vocabulary consistent with cluster.
